You are auditing source code for an INDEPENDENT external code review (client: Telus). You have Bash, Read, Grep tools.

STEP 1 — get your file list. Run:
  /opt/conda/bin/python -c "import json; s=json.load(open('/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/.name_shards.json'))[13]; print(chr(10).join(s))"
That prints your assigned slice as lines of "relpath:START-END" (relative to /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent).

STEP 2 — GENUINELY READ every one of your assigned files/ranges, line by line, using the Read tool with the given offset/limit. Do NOT grep-and-skim. Actually read the code so you catch names a keyword search would miss.

STEP 3 — flag EVERY name (module/file name, class, function/method, variable, constant, enum value) that would look UNPROFESSIONAL in an external code review. This includes:
  - marketing / quality adjectives: honest, real, true, smart, magic, ultimate, enhanced, improved, robust, proper(when boastful), aggressive, killer, awesome, blazing, super, mega, best, clean(when boastful)
  - temporal / version markers: v2/v3/v4, r2/r3, _new, _old, legacy, deprecated, next/nextgen, final(when it means 'this version'), fixed, temp, wip, draft, backup, orig, working, experimental, proto
  - jokes / informal / vague: hack, yolo, stuff, misc, foo, doit, thing, blah, magic numbers named as words
Judge at a STRICT bar: **when in doubt, mark RENAME, not KEEP.** Only mark KEEP for names that are unambiguously standard, descriptive, professional vocabulary or a real domain term (e.g. 'finalize', 'proper_noun', 'evict_older_than', a real product name like 'SmartArt' ONLY if the code truly implements it).

STEP 4 — WRITE your findings by APPENDING TAB-separated rows to /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_13.tsv, one row per flagged name:
  VERDICT<TAB>NAME<TAB>relpath:LINE<TAB>KIND<TAB>WHY_it_reads_unprofessional<TAB>SUGGESTED_professional_name
  (VERDICT = RENAME or KEEP-BUT-NOTED; KIND = file/class/func/var/const/enum)
Use a bash heredoc or printf with real tab characters. If a file range has NO issues, still write one row: NONE<TAB>-<TAB><relpath><TAB>-<TAB>clean<TAB>-.

Return ONLY a one-line summary: "shard 13: <R> renames, <K> kept-noted across <F> files".

BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).

- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

1	"""Phase 2: Tiered loopback dispatcher.
2	
3	Replaces loopback_auto_universal.py with explicit classification:
4	- Tier A: auto-served with schema-valid templates (query gen, dedup, simple schemas)
5	- Tier B: surfaced to operator (source analysis, clustering, verification)
6	- Tier C: surfaced to operator (outline, compose, remediation)
7	
8	Design principles:
9	- No silent skips. Every pending request is classified and logged.
10	- Schema-validated responses (Pydantic) before write. Invalid → keep pending.
11	- Atomic response write: .tmp → fsync → rename.
12	- One-line stdout banner per Tier B/C call.
13	- Shape-drift log: every Tier A template write records template_fingerprint
14	  for Phase 8 comparison against real GLM-5.1 output.
15	
16	Usage: python scripts/loopback_dispatcher.py
17	"""
18	from __future__ import annotations
19	
20	import asyncio
21	import hashlib
22	import json
23	import logging
24	import os
25	import random
26	import signal
27	import sys
28	import time
29	from pathlib import Path
30	from typing import Any
31	
32	ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
33	sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT))
34	os.chdir(ROOT)
35	
36	from dotenv import load_dotenv
37	load_dotenv(override=False)
38	
39	LOOPBACK_DIR = Path(os.getenv("PG_LOOPBACK_DIR", "loopback"))
40	PENDING_DIR = LOOPBACK_DIR / "pending"
41	RESPONSES_DIR = LOOPBACK_DIR / "responses"
42	DONE_DIR = LOOPBACK_DIR / "done"
43	SHAPE_DRIFT_LOG = LOOPBACK_DIR / "shape_drift.jsonl"
44	OPERATOR_QUEUE_LOG = LOOPBACK_DIR / "operator_queue.jsonl"
45	
46	for d in (PENDING_DIR, RESPONSES_DIR, DONE_DIR):
47	    d.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
48	
49	logging.basicConfig(
50	    level=logging.INFO,
51	    format="[%(asctime)s] %(message)s",
52	    datefmt="%H:%M:%S",
53	)
54	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
55	
56	# ───────────────────────────── Classification ─────────────────────────
57	
58	# Tier A: schema name → template generator (name, generator)
59	# Tier B/C: schema name in these sets → surface to operator
60	TIER_B_SCHEMAS = {
61	    "SourceAnalysisBatch",
62	    "EvidenceCardBatch",
63	    "StructuredDataExtraction",
64	    "PageSummaryBatch",
65	    "AgenticRoundAnalysis",
66	    "VerificationBatch",
67	    "GlobalEvidenceAssignment",
68	    "ClusterAssessment",
69	    "BatchClusterResult",
70	    "ClusterPlan",
71	    "GapAnalysis",
72	    "ScopeOutput",
73	    "StormOutlinePlan",
74	    "QuestionDecomposition",
75	}
76	
77	TIER_C_SCHEMAS = {
78	    "ReportOutline",
79	}
80	
81	# Unstructured generate/reason — routed by call_type + prompt content
82	# "generate" with COMPOSE_SYSTEM → Tier C (section compose / remediation)
83	# "generate" with "abstract" system → Tier C (abstract)
84	# "reason" low effort → usually Tier B

87	# ───────────────────────────── Tier A templates ────────────────────────
88	
89	def _seed_from_prompt(prompt: str, system: str) -> int:
90	    """Deterministic seed based on prompt hash — same request same response."""
91	    h = hashlib.sha256((prompt + system).encode("utf-8", errors="ignore")).hexdigest()
92	    return int(h[:8], 16)
93	
94	
95	def _tmpl_query_plan(prompt: str, system: str) -> dict:
96	    """QueryPlan — 20 queries distributed across 9 STORM perspectives."""
97	    random.seed(_seed_from_prompt(prompt, system))
98	    perspectives = [
99	        "Scientific", "Regulatory", "Industry", "Economic",
100	        "Public_Health", "Historical", "Regional",
101	        "Methodological", "Emerging_Trends",
102	    ]
103	    topic_bits = [w for w in prompt.split()[:30] if len(w) > 3 and w.isalpha()]
104	    topic = " ".join(topic_bits[:6]) or "research topic"
105	    queries = []
106	    for i in range(20):
107	        p = perspectives[i % len(perspectives)]
108	        modifier = {
109	            "Scientific": "systematic review meta-analysis",
110	            "Regulatory": "FDA EFSA regulatory guidance",
111	            "Industry": "commercial market report datasheet",
112	            "Economic": "cost-effectiveness QALY economic analysis",
113	            "Public_Health": "WHO CDC population-level outcomes",
114	            "Historical": "foundational study evolution of knowledge",
115	            "Regional": "country-specific geographic comparison",
116	            "Methodological": "study design risk of bias GRADE",
117	            "Emerging_Trends": "preprint 2024 novel technique",
118	        }[p]
119	        queries.append({
120	            "query": f"{topic} {modifier}".strip(),
121	            "intent": f"Find {p.lower()} evidence on the research question",
122	            "source_preference": "academic" if p in ("Scientific", "Methodological") else "both",
123	            "perspective": p,
124	        })
125	    return {
126	        "analysis": f"Decompose research question into multi-perspective queries for: {topic[:60]}",
127	        "search_strategy": "broad",
128	        "sub_queries": queries,
129	        "key_concepts": topic_bits[:10],
130	        "expected_source_types": ["journal_article", "regulatory", "industry_report", "meta_analysis"],
131	    }
132	
133	
134	def _tmpl_seed_query_plan(prompt: str, system: str) -> dict:
135	    """SeedQueryPlan — fallback planner: minimal query list."""
136	    plan = _tmpl_query_plan(prompt, system)
137	    # SeedQueryPlan typically wraps sub_queries in a simpler shape — provide both
138	    return {"queries": [q["query"] for q in plan["sub_queries"]], **plan}
139	
140	
141	def _tmpl_search_refinement(prompt: str, system: str) -> dict:
142	    """SearchRefinement — refine failing queries. Schema: refined queries + rationale."""
143	    random.seed(_seed_from_prompt(prompt, system))
144	    base = " ".join(prompt.split()[:6])
145	    return {
146	        "refined_queries": [
147	            f"{base} meta-analysis",
148	            f"{base} systematic review",
149	            f"{base} randomized controlled trial",
150	        ],
151	        "rationale": "Broaden to include review-level evidence; drop overly specific modifiers.",
152	    }
153	
154	
155	def _tmpl_storm_persona_batch(prompt: str, system: str) -> dict:
156	    """StormPersonaBatch — schema requires personas with perspective, name,
157	    expertise, question_focus (NOT role/description/key_questions).
158	
159	    Validated schema from P5 live run:
160	      StormPersona { perspective, name, expertise, question_focus }
161	      StormPersonaBatch { personas: [StormPersona] }
162	    """
163	    personas = []
164	    for perspective, name, expertise, focus in [
165	        ("Scientific", "Dr. A. Researcher", "Randomized-trial evidence synthesis and clinical endpoint evaluation relevant to the research question.", "Effect sizes from RCTs and meta-analyses with confidence intervals."),
166	        ("Regulatory", "Dr. B. Agency", "Regulatory-science evidence evaluation under FDA/EFSA/Health Canada claim-substantiation frameworks.", "Evidence thresholds and adverse-event signals that trigger consumer advisories."),
167	        ("Industry", "Dr. C. Practitioner", "Translating dietary or intervention evidence into consumer products and behavioral-coaching programs.", "Real-world adherence, retention, and effect sizes vs peer-reviewed magnitudes."),
168	        ("Economic", "Dr. D. Economist", "Cost-effectiveness modeling and QALY analysis for preventive interventions.", "ICERs, QALYs, and opportunity cost vs other interventions."),
169	        ("Public_Health", "Dr. E. Clinician", "Population-level dietary guidance and primary-care counseling across diverse populations.", "Contraindications, adverse events, and equity of applicability."),
170	    ]:
171	        personas.append({
172	            "perspective": perspective,
173	            "name": name,
174	            "expertise": expertise,
175	            "question_focus": focus,
176	        })
177	    return {"personas": personas}
178	
179	
180	def _tmpl_storm_question(prompt: str, system: str) -> dict:
181	    """StormQuestion — schema requires question, search_queries, perspective
182	    (NOT persona_role, follow_up_indicator).
183	
184	    Validated schema from P5 live run:
185	      StormQuestion { question, search_queries[], perspective }
186	    """
187	    # Pull the persona's perspective from the prompt if declared
188	    perspective = "Scientific"
189	    import re as _re
190	    m = _re.search(r"Your perspective:\s*(\w+)", prompt)
191	    if m:
192	        perspective = m.group(1)
193	    return {
194	        "question": "What does the existing evidence show about effectiveness, magnitude, and limitations of the studied intervention compared to standard approaches?",
195	        "search_queries": [
196	            "effectiveness randomized controlled trial systematic review",
197	            "meta-analysis effect size confidence interval",
198	            "adverse events safety contraindications",
199	            "real-world adherence retention long-term outcomes",
200	            "comparison with standard care continuous intervention",
201	        ],
202	        "perspective": perspective,
203	    }
204	
205	
206	def _tmpl_storm_answer(prompt: str, system: str) -> dict:
207	    """StormAnswer — schema requires answer, key_findings, sources_used
208	    (NOT evidence_ids, confidence).
209	
210	    Validated schema from P5 live run:
211	      StormAnswer { answer, key_findings[], sources_used[] }
212	    """
213	    return {
214	        "answer": (
215	            "The retrieved sources were not parsed into specific quantitative findings by the auto-template responder. "
216	            "A full evidence-grounded answer requires the operator LLM to read the provided search results and extract "
217	            "numeric effect estimates, confidence intervals, and methodological caveats."
218	        ),
219	        "key_findings": [
220	            "Auto-template response: specific findings require operator serving.",
221	        ],
222	        "sources_used": [],
223	    }
224	
225	
226	def _tmpl_disambig(prompt: str, system: str) -> dict:
227	    """DisambigResponse — entity disambiguation."""
228	    return {"entity_id": "", "confidence": 0.5, "rationale": "Ambiguous reference; deferring to context."}
229	
230	
231	def _tmpl_sql_query(prompt: str, system: str) -> dict:
232	    """SQLQuery — generate a DB query."""
233	    return {"sql": "SELECT * FROM evidence WHERE 1=1 LIMIT 0", "rationale": "Default no-op query."}
234	
235	
236	TIER_A_TEMPLATES = {
237	    "QueryPlan": _tmpl_query_plan,
238	    "SeedQueryPlan": _tmpl_seed_query_plan,
239	    "SearchRefinement": _tmpl_search_refinement,
240	    "StormPersonaBatch": _tmpl_storm_persona_batch,
241	    "StormQuestion": _tmpl_storm_question,
242	    "StormAnswer": _tmpl_storm_answer,
243	    "DisambigResponse": _tmpl_disambig,
244	    "SQLQuery": _tmpl_sql_query,
245	}

247	
248	# ───────────────────────────── Core dispatcher ─────────────────────────
249	
250	def classify(req: dict) -> tuple[str, str]:
251	    """Return (tier, reason). tier ∈ {'A', 'B', 'C'}."""
252	    schema = req.get("schema_name") or ""
253	    call_type = req.get("call_type", "")
254	    system = req.get("system", "") or ""
255	    prompt = req.get("prompt", "") or ""
256	
257	    if schema in TIER_A_TEMPLATES:
258	        return "A", f"tier-A template for schema={schema}"
259	    if schema in TIER_C_SCHEMAS:
260	        return "C", f"tier-C schema={schema}"
261	    if schema in TIER_B_SCHEMAS:
262	        return "B", f"tier-B schema={schema}"
263	    if schema:
264	        return "B", f"unknown schema={schema} defaulting to tier-B operator"
265	
266	    # Unstructured generate — inspect call_type + system/prompt content
267	    if call_type == "generate":
268	        sl = (system or "").lower()
269	        pl = (prompt or "").lower()
270	        if "academic researcher" in sl or "200-word abstract" in pl:
271	            return "C", "abstract compose"
272	        if "systematic review" in pl and "write section" in pl.lower():
273	            return "C", "section compose"
274	        if "remediation" in pl.lower() or "unsupported" in pl.lower():
275	            return "C", "section remediation re-compose"
276	        return "B", "unstructured generate (default tier-B operator)"
277	    if call_type.startswith("reason"):
278	        return "B", "reason call (default tier-B operator)"
279	
280	    return "B", "default tier-B operator"
281	
282	
283	def _atomic_write(path: Path, data: dict) -> None:
284	    tmp = path.with_suffix(path.suffix + ".tmp")
285	    with open(tmp, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
286	        json.dump(data, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)
287	        f.flush()
288	        try:
289	            os.fsync(f.fileno())
290	        except OSError:
291	            pass  # Windows sometimes rejects fsync on normal files
292	    os.replace(tmp, path)
293	
294	
295	def _validate_template(schema_name: str, obj: dict) -> tuple[bool, str]:
296	    """Validate a template response against the real Pydantic schema.
297	
298	    Scans pg_schemas, claim_extract, and storm_interviews modules for the
299	    named class (STORM schemas live in storm_interviews, not pg_schemas).
300	    """
301	    _candidate_modules = [
302	        "src.polaris_graph.schemas",
303	        "src.polaris_graph.wiki.mesh.claim_extract",
304	        "src.polaris_graph.agents.storm_interviews",
305	    ]
306	    cls = None
307	    last_err = ""
308	    for modname in _candidate_modules:
309	        try:
310	            import importlib
311	            mod = importlib.import_module(modname)
312	            cand = getattr(mod, schema_name, None)
313	            if cand is not None:
314	                cls = cand
315	                break
316	        except Exception as exc:
317	            last_err = str(exc)
318	    if cls is None:
319	        return False, f"schema class {schema_name} not found (last import error: {last_err})"
320	
321	    try:
322	        cls.model_validate(obj)
323	        return True, "OK"
324	    except Exception as exc:
325	        return False, f"validation failed: {str(exc)[:200]}"
326	
327	
328	def _log_shape_drift(req_id: str, schema: str, fingerprint: str, obj: dict) -> None:
329	    """Append a shape-drift record for Phase 8 comparison."""
330	    entry = {
331	        "ts": time.time(),
332	        "req_id": req_id,
333	        "schema": schema,
334	        "template_fingerprint": fingerprint,
335	        "response_keys": list(obj.keys())[:20],
336	    }
337	    with open(SHAPE_DRIFT_LOG, "a", encoding="utf-8") as f:
338	        f.write(json.dumps(entry) + "\n")
339	
340	
341	def _log_operator_queue(req_id: str, tier: str, schema: str, reason: str, prompt_head: str) -> None:
342	    entry = {
343	        "ts": time.time(),
344	        "req_id": req_id,
345	        "tier": tier,
346	        "schema": schema,
347	        "reason": reason,
348	        "prompt_head": prompt_head[:160],
349	    }
350	    with open(OPERATOR_QUEUE_LOG, "a", encoding="utf-8") as f:
351	        f.write(json.dumps(entry) + "\n")
352	
353	
354	async def serve_tier_a(req: dict, req_path: Path) -> bool:
355	    """Auto-serve a Tier A request. Returns True if served successfully."""
356	    schema = req.get("schema_name", "")
357	    req_id = req.get("request_id", req_path.stem.replace("req_", ""))
358	
359	    generator = TIER_A_TEMPLATES[schema]
360	    obj = generator(req.get("prompt", ""), req.get("system", ""))
361	
362	    ok, msg = _validate_template(schema, obj)
363	    if not ok:
364	        logger.warning("[TIER A] schema=%s template rejected: %s. Leaving pending.", schema, msg)
365	        return False
366	
367	    fingerprint = hashlib.sha256(
368	        json.dumps(obj, sort_keys=True).encode("utf-8")
369	    ).hexdigest()[:12]
370	    _log_shape_drift(req_id, schema, fingerprint, obj)
371	
372	    response = {
373	        "content": json.dumps(obj, ensure_ascii=False),
374	        "reasoning": "",
375	        "input_tokens": max(1, len(req.get("prompt", "")) // 4),
376	        "output_tokens": max(1, len(json.dumps(obj)) // 4),
377	        "_template_fingerprint": fingerprint,
378	    }
379	    resp_path = RESPONSES_DIR / f"resp_{req_id}.json"
380	    _atomic_write(resp_path, response)
381	    logger.info("[TIER A] served req_%s schema=%s fingerprint=%s", req_id, schema, fingerprint)
382	    return True
383	
384	
385	def announce_operator(req: dict, req_path: Path, tier: str, reason: str) -> None:
386	    """Print an operator-visible banner for Tier B/C calls."""
387	    req_id = req.get("request_id", req_path.stem.replace("req_", ""))
388	    schema = req.get("schema_name") or "(unstructured)"
389	    call_type = req.get("call_type", "")
390	    prompt = req.get("prompt", "")
391	
392	    _log_operator_queue(req_id, tier, schema, reason, prompt)
393	
394	    banner = (
395	        f"\n{'='*72}\n"
396	        f"  OPERATOR NEEDED — TIER {tier}\n"
397	        f"  req_id:    {req_id}\n"
398	        f"  call_type: {call_type}\n"
399	        f"  schema:    {schema}\n"
400	        f"  reason:    {reason}\n"
401	        f"  prompt_head: {prompt[:160]}...\n"
402	        f"  file:      {req_path}\n"
403	        f"  To serve: inspect {req_path}, write loopback/responses/resp_{req_id}.json\n"
404	        f"{'='*72}\n"
405	    )
406	    print(banner, flush=True)
407	    logger.info("[TIER %s] OPERATOR req_%s schema=%s reason=%s", tier, req_id, schema, reason)
408	
409	
410	async def process_pending_once(seen: set[str]) -> int:
411	    """Scan pending/ once. Serve Tier A, announce Tier B/C. Return count processed."""
412	    count = 0
413	    for req_path in sorted(PENDING_DIR.glob("req_*.json")):
414	        if req_path.stem in seen:
415	            continue
416	        try:
417	            with open(req_path, encoding="utf-8") as f:
418	                req = json.load(f)
419	        except (json.JSONDecodeError, PermissionError, OSError):
420	            continue  # still being written; try next loop
421	
422	        tier, reason = classify(req)
423	
424	        if tier == "A":
425	            served = await serve_tier_a(req, req_path)
426	            if served:
427	                seen.add(req_path.stem)
428	                count += 1
429	            # if not served, leave pending (validator rejected) — operator can handle
430	            else:
431	                announce_operator(req, req_path, tier="B",
432	                                  reason=f"Tier A validator rejected: {reason}")
433	                seen.add(req_path.stem)
434	        else:
435	            announce_operator(req, req_path, tier=tier, reason=reason)
436	            seen.add(req_path.stem)
437	            count += 1
438	    return count
439	
440	
441	_shutdown = asyncio.Event()
442	
443	
444	def _handle_signal(signum, frame):
445	    logger.info("Signal %d received — initiating graceful shutdown", signum)
446	    _shutdown.set()
447	
448	
449	async def main() -> int:
450	    signal.signal(signal.SIGINT, _handle_signal)
451	    try:
452	        signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, _handle_signal)
453	    except (AttributeError, ValueError):
454	        pass  # Windows
455	
456	    poll_interval = float(os.getenv("PG_LOOPBACK_POLL_SEC", "1.0"))
457	    logger.info(
458	        "Loopback dispatcher ready. pending=%s responses=%s done=%s poll=%.1fs",
459	        PENDING_DIR, RESPONSES_DIR, DONE_DIR, poll_interval,
460	    )
461	    logger.info(
462	        "Tier A schemas: %s",
463	        ", ".join(sorted(TIER_A_TEMPLATES.keys())),
464	    )
465	
466	    seen: set[str] = set()
467	    idle_ticks = 0
468	    while not _shutdown.is_set():
469	        n = await process_pending_once(seen)
470	        if n > 0:
471	            idle_ticks = 0
472	        else:
473	            idle_ticks += 1
474	            if idle_ticks % 30 == 0:
475	                logger.info("idle (%ds) — %d requests served so far", int(idle_ticks * poll_interval), len(seen))
476	        try:
477	            await asyncio.wait_for(_shutdown.wait(), timeout=poll_interval)
478	            break
479	        except asyncio.TimeoutError:
480	            continue
481	
482	    logger.info("Dispatcher shutdown. Total classified: %d", len(seen))
483	    return 0
484	
485	
486	if __name__ == "__main__":
487	    sys.stdout.reconfigure(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
488	    sys.exit(asyncio.run(main()))

1	"""Run all 10 loopback vectors sequentially, collect D3 metrics.
2	
3	Usage: python scripts/loopback_run_all_10.py
4	
5	Requires loopback_auto_universal.py running in background.
6	"""
7	import asyncio
8	import json
9	import os
10	import re
11	import sys
12	import time
13	from pathlib import Path
14	
15	ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
16	sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT))
17	os.chdir(ROOT)
18	
19	TOPICS_FILE = ROOT / "scripts" / "pg_loopback_topics.json"
20	OUTPUT_DIR = ROOT / "outputs" / "polaris_graph"
21	
22	
23	def load_topics() -> list[dict]:
24	    with open(TOPICS_FILE, encoding="utf-8") as f:
25	        return json.load(f)["topics"]
26	
27	
28	def audit_d3(vector_id: str) -> dict:
29	    """Audit D3 URL canonicalization for one completed vector."""
30	    json_path = OUTPUT_DIR / f"{vector_id}.json"
31	    md_path = OUTPUT_DIR / f"{vector_id}_report.md"
32	
33	    if not json_path.exists():
34	        return {"vector_id": vector_id, "status": "MISSING_JSON"}
35	    if not md_path.exists():
36	        return {"vector_id": vector_id, "status": "MISSING_REPORT"}
37	
38	    with open(json_path, encoding="utf-8") as f:
39	        data = json.load(f)
40	    with open(md_path, encoding="utf-8") as f:
41	        report = f.read()
42	
43	    bib = data.get("bibliography", [])
44	    urls = [b.get("url", "") for b in bib]
45	    distinct_urls = len(set(urls))
46	    total_bib = len(bib)
47	
48	    # Normalize for near-duplicate detection
49	    def norm(u):
50	        return u.lower().rstrip("/").replace("www.", "").replace("https://", "").replace("http://", "").split("?")[0]
51	    norm_urls = [norm(u) for u in urls]
52	    from collections import Counter
53	    dupes = [(u, c) for u, c in Counter(norm_urls).items() if c > 1]
54	
55	    # Citation cross-check
56	    idx = report.find("## References")
57	    prose = report[:idx] if idx > 0 else report
58	    bib_text = report[idx:] if idx > 0 else ""
59	    prose_cites = set(int(m.group(1)) for m in re.finditer(r"\[(\d+)\]", prose))
60	    bib_ids = set(int(m.group(1)) for m in re.finditer(r"^\[(\d+)\] ", bib_text, re.MULTILINE))
61	    phantom = sorted(prose_cites - bib_ids)
62	    orphaned = sorted(bib_ids - prose_cites)
63	
64	    # Hallucination signals
65	    halluc_kw = ["Zhong", "NHANES", "PMID", "Science Media Centre", "Burg-Peet"]
66	    halluc_hits = {kw: prose.count(kw) for kw in halluc_kw if prose.count(kw) > 0}
67	
68	    ha = data.get("hallucination_audit", [])
69	    ha_populated = len(ha) > 0 if isinstance(ha, list) else bool(ha)
70	
71	    qm = data.get("quality_metrics", {})
72	
73	    return {
74	        "vector_id": vector_id,
75	        "status": data.get("status", "unknown"),
76	        "bib_entries": total_bib,
77	        "distinct_urls": distinct_urls,
78	        "near_dupes": dupes,
79	        "prose_cites": sorted(prose_cites),
80	        "phantom_cites": phantom,
81	        "orphaned_bib": orphaned,
82	        "halluc_signals": halluc_hits,
83	        "halluc_audit_populated": ha_populated,
84	        "words": qm.get("total_words", 0),
85	        "citations": qm.get("total_citations", 0),
86	        "faithfulness": data.get("faithfulness_score", 0),
87	    }
88	
89	
90	async def run_one(topic: dict) -> dict:
91	    vid = topic["vector_id"]
92	    query = topic["query"]
93	    app = topic.get("application", "Systematic Review")
94	    region = topic.get("region", "GLOBAL")
95	
96	    print(f"\n{'='*60}")
97	    print(f"  {vid}: {query[:60]}...")
98	    print(f"{'='*60}")
99	
100	    start = time.time()
101	
102	    # Set env vars matching pg_loopback_full_scale.py
103	    os.environ["PG_LOOPBACK_MODE"] = "1"
104	    os.environ["PG_MAX_ITERATIONS"] = "4"
105	    os.environ["PG_QUERIES_PER_VECTOR"] = "30"
106	    os.environ["PG_AGENTIC_MAX_ROUNDS"] = "5"
107	    os.environ["PG_AGENTIC_MIN_ROUNDS"] = "2"
108	    os.environ["PG_AGENTIC_PAGES_PER_ROUND"] = "6"
109	    os.environ["PG_MAX_SOURCES_TO_ANALYZE"] = "60"
110	    os.environ["PG_MAX_EVIDENCE_TO_EXTRACT"] = "400"
111	    os.environ["PG_MAX_EVIDENCE_FOR_VERIFY"] = "2000"
112	    os.environ["PG_MAX_EVIDENCE_FOR_SYNTHESIS"] = "1000"
113	    os.environ["PG_VERIFY_BATCH_SIZE"] = "10"
114	    os.environ["PG_STORM_ROUNDS_PER_PERSPECTIVE"] = "3"
115	    os.environ["PG_STORM_CONCURRENCY"] = "2"
116	    os.environ["PG_MAX_OUTLINE_SECTIONS"] = "8"
117	    os.environ["PG_MAX_SECTIONS"] = "8"
118	    os.environ["PG_SECTION_WRITE_CONCURRENCY"] = "2"
119	    os.environ["PG_SYNTHESIS_MAX_EXPANSION_PASSES"] = "2"
120	    os.environ["PG_MAX_EXECUTION_MINUTES"] = "1440"
121	    os.environ["PG_BUDGET_GUARD_USD"] = "40"
122	    # All timeouts large for loopback
123	    for k in ["PG_ANALYSIS_BATCH_TIMEOUT", "PG_STORM_INTERVIEW_TIMEOUT",
124	              "PG_AGENTIC_ANALYSIS_TIMEOUT_SECONDS", "PG_VERIFY_BATCH_TIMEOUT",
125	              "PG_VERIFY_PER_CALL_TIMEOUT", "PG_CLUSTER_BATCH_TIMEOUT",
126	              "PG_SECTION_WRITE_TIMEOUT", "PG_PLANNER_TIMEOUT",
127	              "PG_REACT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS", "PG_WRITE_TIMEOUT",
128	              "PG_STRUCTURED_DATA_TOTAL_TIMEOUT", "PG_LLM_TIMEOUT_SECONDS",
129	              "PG_LLM_LONG_TIMEOUT_SECONDS", "PG_WIKI_OUTLINE_TIMEOUT",
130	              "PG_WIKI_ABSTRACT_TIMEOUT", "PG_WIKI_COMPOSE_TIMEOUT",
131	              "PG_QUESTION_DECOMP_TIMEOUT", "PG_OUTLINE_TIMEOUT",
132	              "PG_MOST_TOTAL_TIMEOUT"]:
133	        os.environ[k] = "7200"
134	    os.environ["PG_VERIFY_GATHER_TIMEOUT"] = "28800"
135	    os.environ["PG_AGENTIC_MAX_TIME_SECONDS"] = "72000"
136	    os.environ["PG_STORM_MAX_TIME_SECONDS"] = "72000"
137	    os.environ["PG_AGENTIC_FETCH_TIMEOUT"] = "180"
138	    os.environ["PG_LOOPBACK_TIMEOUT_SEC"] = "14400"
139	
140	    from dotenv import load_dotenv
141	    load_dotenv(override=False)
142	
143	    try:
144	        from src.polaris_graph.graph import build_and_run
145	        result = await build_and_run(
146	            vector_id=vid, query=query, application=app,
147	            region=region, stage=1, max_iterations=4, max_execution_minutes=1440,
148	        )
149	        elapsed = time.time() - start
150	        status = result.get("status", "unknown")
151	        print(f"  {vid} completed in {elapsed:.0f}s ({elapsed/60:.1f}min) — status={status}")
152	    except Exception as exc:
153	        elapsed = time.time() - start
154	        print(f"  {vid} CRASHED after {elapsed:.0f}s: {exc}")
155	        import traceback
156	        traceback.print_exc()
157	
158	    return audit_d3(vid)
159	
160	
161	async def main():
162	    topics = load_topics()
163	    print(f"Loaded {len(topics)} topics from {TOPICS_FILE}")
164	
165	    results = []
166	    for topic in topics:
167	        r = await run_one(topic)
168	        results.append(r)
169	        print(f"  D3 audit: {r['vector_id']} — bib={r.get('bib_entries')}, "
170	              f"distinct={r.get('distinct_urls')}, dupes={len(r.get('near_dupes', []))}, "
171	              f"phantom={r.get('phantom_cites')}, halluc={r.get('halluc_signals')}, "
172	              f"audit_populated={r.get('halluc_audit_populated')}")
173	
174	    # Summary
175	    print(f"\n{'='*60}")
176	    print(f"  10-VECTOR D3 SUMMARY")
177	    print(f"{'='*60}")
178	    for r in results:
179	        status = "PASS" if not r.get("near_dupes") and not r.get("phantom_cites") and not r.get("halluc_signals") else "FAIL"
180	        print(f"  {r['vector_id']}: {status} — bib={r.get('bib_entries',0)}, dupes={len(r.get('near_dupes',[]))}, "
181	              f"phantom={len(r.get('phantom_cites',[]))}, halluc={len(r.get('halluc_signals',{}))}, "
182	              f"audit={r.get('halluc_audit_populated')}")
183	
184	    # Write results
185	    out_path = ROOT / "outputs" / "loopback_10_vector_d3_audit.json"
186	    with open(out_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
187	        json.dump(results, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)
188	    print(f"\nResults written to {out_path}")
189	
190	
191	if __name__ == "__main__":
192	    sys.stdout.reconfigure(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
193	    asyncio.run(main())

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""GHOST MEASUREMENT — basket UNDER-UTILIZATION, isolated deterministically.
3	
4	Builds REAL, verifiable ClaimBaskets from the corrected corpus (evidence_id + verbatim
5	direct_quote so ``build_verified_span_draft`` emits a real [#ev]-tokened sentence that
6	re-passes the UNCHANGED strict_verify), a FIXED thematic outline, and a sparse per-section
7	primary assignment (mimicking the live LLM outline that assigns only a handful of ev_ids
8	per section). Then measures, with NO LLM and NO network (PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT off),
9	the compose-side utilization A/B:
10	
11	  ARM A  route_all OFF (banked default)  -> baskets rendered, verified sentences, words
12	  ARM B  route_all ON  (PG_ROUTE_ALL_BASKETS=1) -> same
13	
14	The ONLY variable between arms is ``PG_ROUTE_ALL_BASKETS`` (and its companion off-topic
15	delete gate), so the delta is a CLEAN, confound-free measurement of the utilization lever
16	(the live agentic run varies its outline+retrieval between runs, which confounds a live
17	A/B — see WHEEL_PROGRESS STEP 3). Faithfulness is byte-identical between arms: every
18	rendered sentence is a verbatim corpus span carrying its own [#ev] token.
19	"""
20	from __future__ import annotations
21	
22	import json
23	import os
24	import re
25	import sys
26	from pathlib import Path
27	
28	_REPO = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
29	sys.path.insert(0, str(_REPO))
30	
31	# Deterministic verification only — no entailment judge, no network.
32	os.environ.pop("PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT", None)
33	
34	from src.polaris_graph.generator.provenance_generator import verify_sentence_provenance  # noqa: E402
35	from src.polaris_graph.generator.verified_compose import (  # noqa: E402
36	    _compose_section_per_basket,
37	    _section_baskets_for_compose,
38	    build_verified_span_draft,
39	    route_orphan_baskets_to_section_plans,
40	)
41	from src.polaris_graph.generator.multi_section_generator import SectionPlan  # noqa: E402
42	from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.credibility_pass import (  # noqa: E402
43	    BasketMember,
44	    ClaimBasket,
45	    CredibilityAnalysis,
46	    MEMBER_TIER_ENTAILMENT_VERIFIED,
47	)
48	
49	_STOP = frozenset(
50	    "the and for that this with from have has had was were are been will would could "
51	    "should their there these those which while when where what who whom into than then "
52	    "such also not but its our your his her they them some more most over under between "
53	    "among per via about each any all study studies effect effects".split()
54	)
55	_WORD = re.compile(r"[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9\-]{2,}")
56	
57	
58	def _cw(text: str) -> set:
59	    return {w.lower() for w in _WORD.findall(text or "") if w.lower() not in _STOP}
60	
61	
62	# A FIXED thematic outline (titles+focus mirror the banked step3_control report so the
63	# section->basket topical routing is realistic; NOTHING task-specific is hardcoded into the
64	# compose engine — these are just plan objects handed to the SAME production functions).
65	_OUTLINE = [
66	    ("Task-Based Frameworks and Displacement-Reinstatement",
67	     "automation tasks displacement reinstatement labor demand framework"),
68	    ("Occupational Exposure and Susceptibility to AI",
69	     "occupational exposure susceptibility generative AI large language models tasks"),
70	    ("Empirical Evidence on Employment and Labor Demand",
71	     "employment labor demand jobs empirical estimates workers hiring"),
72	    ("Productivity Effects of Generative AI",
73	     "productivity augmentation output performance generative AI workers gains"),
74	    ("Wage Inequality, Skill Demand, and Labor Polarization",
75	     "wage inequality skill demand polarization earnings distribution workers"),
76	    ("Policy Implications and Frameworks for Managing Disruption",
77	     "policy regulation reskilling training transition governance frameworks"),
78	    ("Cross-Study Synthesis and Contradictions",
79	     "cross study synthesis agreement disagreement contradiction convergence conflict findings"),
80	]
81	
82	
83	def _build_baskets(corpus):
84	    ev = corpus["evidence"]
85	    pool = {}
86	    for r in ev:
87	        eid = str(r.get("evidence_id") or "")
88	        if eid:
89	            pool[eid] = r
90	    baskets = []
91	    for cl in corpus.get("finding_clusters") or []:
92	        try:
93	            midx = json.loads(cl["member_indices"]) if isinstance(cl["member_indices"], str) else cl["member_indices"]
94	        except Exception:
95	            continue
96	        rep = int(cl.get("representative_index", midx[0] if midx else 0))
97	        members = []
98	        seen = set()
99	        for i in midx:
100	            if not (0 <= i < len(ev)):
101	                continue
102	            row = ev[i]
103	            eid = str(row.get("evidence_id") or "")
104	            if not eid or eid in seen:
105	                continue
106	            seen.add(eid)
107	            quote = str(row.get("direct_quote") or row.get("statement") or "").strip()
108	            if not quote:
109	                continue
110	            members.append(BasketMember(
111	                evidence_id=eid, source_url=str(row.get("source_url") or ""),
112	                source_tier=str(row.get("tier") or ""), origin_cluster_id=eid,
113	                credibility_weight=1.0, authority_score=1.0,
114	                span=(0, len(quote)), direct_quote=quote, span_verdict="SUPPORTS",
115	                member_tier=MEMBER_TIER_ENTAILMENT_VERIFIED,
116	            ))
117	        if not members:
118	            continue
119	        rep_row = ev[rep] if 0 <= rep < len(ev) else ev[midx[0]]
120	        claim = str(rep_row.get("statement") or rep_row.get("title") or "").strip()
121	        baskets.append(ClaimBasket(
122	            claim_cluster_id=str(cl.get("claim_group_id") or f"cg_{rep}"),
123	            claim_text=claim, subject=claim[:120], predicate="",
124	            supporting_members=members, refuter_cluster_ids=(),
125	            weight_mass=float(len(members)), total_clustered_origin_count=len(members),
126	            verified_support_origin_count=len(members),
127	            basket_verdict="full",
128	        ))
129	    return pool, baskets
130	
131	
132	def _assign_primary(baskets, pool, cap_per_section):
133	    """Sparse per-section primary assignment: each basket's rep member is scored against every
134	    section by claim/title content-word overlap; the top ``cap_per_section`` baskets per section
135	    (highest overlap) contribute their member ev_ids. Mimics the live LLM outline that lists only
136	    a handful of ev_ids per section (the throttle that stranded ~90% of baskets)."""
137	    plans = [SectionPlan(title=t, focus=f, ev_ids=[]) for t, f in _OUTLINE]
138	    plan_words = [_cw(t + " " + f) for t, f in _OUTLINE]
139	    scored = [[] for _ in plans]
140	    for b in baskets:
141	        bw = _cw(b.claim_text)
142	        best_i, best_o = -1, 0
143	        for i, pw in enumerate(plan_words):
144	            o = len(bw & pw)
145	            if o > best_o:
146	                best_o, best_i = o, i
147	        if best_i >= 0:
148	            scored[best_i].append((best_o, b))
149	    for i, plan in enumerate(plans):
150	        top = sorted(scored[i], key=lambda x: x[0], reverse=True)[:cap_per_section]
151	        ids = []
152	        for _o, b in top:
153	            for m in b.supporting_members:
154	                if m.evidence_id not in ids:
155	                    ids.append(m.evidence_id)
156	        plan.ev_ids = ids
157	    return plans
158	
159	
160	def _writer_null(_basket, _pool):
161	    return ""  # force the production K-span verbatim fallback (deterministic)
162	
163	
164	def _compose_and_count(plans, cred, pool):
165	    rendered_baskets = set()
166	    total_sentences = 0
167	    total_words = 0
168	    per_section = []
169	    for plan in plans:
170	        sb = _section_baskets_for_compose(plan, cred, evidence_pool=pool)
171	        composed = _compose_section_per_basket(
172	            sb, pool, writer_fn=_writer_null, verify_fn=verify_sentence_provenance,
173	        )
174	        sec_sents = 0
175	        sec_words = 0
176	        sec_rendered = 0
177	        for b, text in zip(sb, composed):
178	            if text and text.strip():
179	                sec_rendered += 1
180	                rendered_baskets.add(b.claim_cluster_id)
181	                n = len([s for s in re.split(r"(?<=[.!?])\s+", text.strip()) if s.strip()])
182	                sec_sents += n
183	                sec_words += len(text.split())
184	        total_sentences += sec_sents
185	        total_words += sec_words
186	        per_section.append((plan.title, len(sb), sec_rendered, sec_sents, sec_words))
187	    return {
188	        "baskets_rendered": len(rendered_baskets),
189	        "sentences": total_sentences,
190	        "words": total_words,
191	        "per_section": per_section,
192	    }
193	
194	
195	def main():
196	    corpus_path = _REPO / "data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.corrected.json"
197	    corpus = json.loads(corpus_path.read_text())
198	    pool, baskets = _build_baskets(corpus)
199	    cap = int(os.getenv("CAP_PER_SECTION", "6"))
200	    print(f"corpus baskets built (verifiable) = {len(baskets)} / {len(corpus.get('finding_clusters') or [])}"
201	          f"  | evidence rows = {len(pool)}  | cap_per_section = {cap}")
202	
203	    cred = CredibilityAnalysis(
204	        credibility_by_evidence={}, origin_by_evidence={}, claims=[], edges=[],
205	        weight_mass=[], baskets=baskets,
206	    )
207	
208	    # ARM A — route_all OFF
209	    os.environ["PG_ROUTE_ALL_BASKETS"] = "0"
210	    plans_a = _assign_primary(baskets, pool, cap)
211	    a = _compose_and_count(plans_a, cred, pool)
212	
213	    # ARM B — route_all ON (companion off-topic delete gate uses corpus judge dispositions; none here)
214	    os.environ["PG_ROUTE_ALL_BASKETS"] = "1"
215	    plans_b = _assign_primary(baskets, pool, cap)
216	    plans_b = route_orphan_baskets_to_section_plans(
217	        plans_b, cred, section_plan_cls=SectionPlan,
218	    )
219	    b = _compose_and_count(plans_b, cred, pool)
220	
221	    def _fmt(tag, r):
222	        print(f"\n=== ARM {tag} ===")
223	        print(f"  baskets_rendered = {r['baskets_rendered']}   verified_sentences = {r['sentences']}   words = {r['words']}")
224	        for title, nsb, nren, ns, nw in r["per_section"]:
225	            print(f"    - {title[:46]:46}  baskets_in_section={nsb:3}  rendered={nren:3}  sents={ns:3}  words={nw:4}")
226	
227	    _fmt("A route_all=OFF", a)
228	    _fmt("B route_all=ON ", b)
229	    print("\n=== DELTA (B - A) ===")
230	    print(f"  baskets_rendered: {a['baskets_rendered']} -> {b['baskets_rendered']}  (+{b['baskets_rendered']-a['baskets_rendered']})")
231	    print(f"  verified_sentences: {a['sentences']} -> {b['sentences']}  (+{b['sentences']-a['sentences']})")
232	    print(f"  words: {a['words']} -> {b['words']}  (+{b['words']-a['words']})")
233	    out = {"corpus_baskets": len(baskets), "cap_per_section": cap, "arm_a_route_off": a, "arm_b_route_on": b}
234	    (_REPO / "outputs").mkdir(exist_ok=True)
235	    (_REPO / "outputs" / "utilization_route_all_measure.json").write_text(json.dumps(out, indent=2))
236	    print("\nWROTE outputs/utilization_route_all_measure.json")
237	    return 0
238	
239	
240	if __name__ == "__main__":
241	    raise SystemExit(main())

1	"""Monitor POLARIS frontend via Playwright — screenshots every 45s through tunnel."""
2	import json
3	import sys
4	import time
5	import urllib.request
6	from datetime import datetime
7	from pathlib import Path
8	
9	from playwright.sync_api import sync_playwright
10	
11	SCREENSHOTS_DIR = Path("C:/POLARIS/outputs/monitor_screenshots")
12	SCREENSHOTS_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
13	
14	# Use tunnel URL if provided, otherwise localhost
15	BASE_URL = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else "http://localhost:8765"
16	INTERVAL = 45
17	
18	
19	LOCAL_URL = "http://localhost:8765"
20	
21	
22	def fetch_status():
23	    try:
24	        with urllib.request.urlopen(LOCAL_URL + "/api/research/status", timeout=10) as r:
25	            return json.loads(r.read().decode("utf-8"))
26	    except Exception:
27	        return {"running": False, "error": "unreachable"}
28	
29	
30	def main():
31	    print("=" * 70)
32	    print("  POLARIS Frontend Monitor — Playwright screenshots every %ds" % INTERVAL)
33	    print("  URL: %s" % BASE_URL)
34	    print("  Screenshots: %s" % SCREENSHOTS_DIR)
35	    print("=" * 70)
36	
37	    with sync_playwright() as p:
38	        browser = p.chromium.launch(headless=True)
39	        context = browser.new_context(viewport={"width": 1920, "height": 1080})
40	        page = context.new_page()
41	
42	        # Collect JS errors
43	        js_errors = []
44	        page.on("pageerror", lambda e: js_errors.append(str(e)))
45	
46	        # Initial load
47	        print("[INIT] Loading dashboard...")
48	        page.goto(BASE_URL, wait_until="domcontentloaded", timeout=60000)
49	        page.wait_for_timeout(3000)  # Let hydration + SSE connect
50	
51	        shot_num = 0
52	        while True:
53	            now = datetime.now().strftime("%H%M%S")
54	            status = fetch_status()
55	            running = status.get("running", False)
56	
57	            # Grab frontend state
58	            try:
59	                fe_state = page.evaluate("""() => {
60	                    if (typeof state === 'undefined') return {error: 'no state'};
61	                    return {
62	                        connected: state.connected,
63	                        eventCount: state.eventCount,
64	                        evidence: state.evidence,
65	                        sources: state.sources ? state.sources.size : 0,
66	                        faithfulness: state.faithfulness,
67	                        words: state.words,
68	                        citations: state.citations,
69	                        cost: state.cost,
70	                        iteration: state.iteration,
71	                        pipelineActive: state.pipelineActive,
72	                        pipelineComplete: state.pipelineComplete,
73	                        currentNode: state.currentNode,
74	                        bibliography: state.bibliography ? state.bibliography.length : 0,
75	                    };
76	                }""")
77	            except Exception as e:
78	                fe_state = {"error": str(e)[:100]}
79	
80	            # Screenshot
81	            shot_name = "monitor_%03d_%s.png" % (shot_num, now)
82	            shot_path = SCREENSHOTS_DIR / shot_name
83	            page.screenshot(path=str(shot_path), full_page=False)
84	
85	            # Report
86	            be_running = "RUN" if running else "DONE"
87	            fe_conn = fe_state.get("connected", "?")
88	            fe_ev = fe_state.get("eventCount", "?")
89	            fe_evidence = fe_state.get("evidence", "?")
90	            fe_sources = fe_state.get("sources", "?")
91	            fe_faith = fe_state.get("faithfulness", "?")
92	            fe_words = fe_state.get("words", "?")
93	            fe_cite = fe_state.get("citations", "?")
94	            fe_active = fe_state.get("pipelineActive", "?")
95	            fe_complete = fe_state.get("pipelineComplete", "?")
96	            fe_node = fe_state.get("currentNode", "?")
97	
98	            new_errors = len(js_errors)
99	
100	            print(
101	                "[%s] #%d | BE=%s | FE: conn=%s active=%s node=%s | "
102	                "events=%s ev=%s src=%s faith=%s words=%s cite=%s | "
103	                "js_errors=%d | %s"
104	                % (
105	                    now, shot_num, be_running, fe_conn, fe_active, fe_node,
106	                    fe_ev, fe_evidence, fe_sources, fe_faith, fe_words, fe_cite,
107	                    new_errors, shot_name,
108	                )
109	            )
110	
111	            if new_errors > 0:
112	                for err in js_errors[-3:]:
113	                    print("  JS ERROR: %s" % err[:200])
114	
115	            shot_num += 1
116	
117	            # Check if done
118	            if not running:
119	                # Take final screenshot
120	                page.wait_for_timeout(3000)
121	                final_name = "monitor_FINAL_%s.png" % now
122	                page.screenshot(path=str(SCREENSHOTS_DIR / final_name), full_page=False)
123	                print("\n  PIPELINE FINISHED — final screenshot: %s" % final_name)
124	
125	                # Check frontend state matches
126	                if fe_state.get("pipelineComplete"):
127	                    print("  Frontend: pipelineComplete=True (GOOD)")
128	                elif fe_state.get("pipelineActive"):
129	                    print("  WARNING: Frontend still shows pipelineActive=True (stale)")
130	                break
131	
132	            time.sleep(INTERVAL)
133	
134	        browser.close()
135	
136	    print("=" * 70)
137	    print("  Frontend monitor stopped. %d screenshots saved." % (shot_num + 1))
138	    print("=" * 70)
139	
140	
141	if __name__ == "__main__":
142	    main()

1	"""
2	Migrate pre-HONEST-REBUILD POLARIS run JSONs to the new schema.
3	
4	Plan: C:/Users/msn/.claude/plans/lovely-finding-firefly.md Phase 1e.
5	
6	Pre-rebuild schema:
7	    {
8	        "faithfulness_score": 1.0,          # self-graded, survivorship-biased
9	        "quality_metrics": {
10	            "faithfulness_score": 1.0,      # ditto
11	            ...
12	        },
13	        "hallucination_audit": [...],       # NLI post-synthesis audit
14	        ...
15	    }
16	
17	Post-rebuild schema:
18	    {
19	        "mode_label": "Legacy run, pre-honest-rebuild",
20	        "evaluator_output": {
21	            "legacy_faithfulness": 1.0,
22	            "legacy_note": "LEGACY METRIC, NOT COMPARABLE TO CURRENT OUTPUT",
23	            "external_evaluator_grade": null,   # Phase 5 will fill this in
24	        },
25	        "quality_metrics": {
26	            # faithfulness_score renamed to _legacy_faithfulness_score
27	            ...
28	        },
29	        "_legacy_hallucination_audit": [...],   # preserved, not re-used
30	        ...
31	    }
32	
33	Behavior:
34	- Idempotent: running twice yields the same output as running once.
35	- Original files preserved at outputs/_archive_pre_rebuild/<filename>.
36	- Emits a manifest at outputs/_archive_pre_rebuild/_migration_manifest.json
37	  listing processed files, skipped files, and any errors.
38	- Does NOT re-score or re-evaluate any content. Only renames fields and
39	  adds the legacy marker. Phase 5's external evaluator handles scoring.
40	
41	Usage:
42	    python scripts/migrate_old_runs.py
43	    python scripts/migrate_old_runs.py --dry-run
44	    python scripts/migrate_old_runs.py --path outputs/polaris_graph
45	"""
46	
47	from __future__ import annotations
48	
49	import argparse
50	import json
51	import shutil
52	import sys
53	from datetime import datetime, timezone
54	from pathlib import Path
55	from typing import Any
56	
57	
58	LEGACY_NOTE = "LEGACY METRIC, NOT COMPARABLE TO CURRENT OUTPUT"
59	LEGACY_MODE_LABEL = "Legacy run, pre-honest-rebuild"
60	MIGRATION_MARKER = "_honest_rebuild_migration"
61	
62	
63	def _is_already_migrated(data: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
64	    """Check if the file has already been migrated (idempotency guard)."""
65	    return data.get(MIGRATION_MARKER) is not None
66	
67	
68	def _needs_migration(data: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
69	    """Check if the file contains pre-rebuild schema."""
70	    if "faithfulness_score" in data:
71	        return True
72	    qm = data.get("quality_metrics") or {}
73	    if "faithfulness_score" in qm:
74	        return True
75	    if "hallucination_audit" in data:
76	        return True
77	    return False
78	
79	
80	def migrate_record(data: dict[str, Any]) -> tuple[dict[str, Any], list[str]]:
81	    """Return a migrated copy of the record and a list of changes applied.
82	
83	    Does not mutate the input.
84	    """
85	    changes: list[str] = []
86	    out = dict(data)  # shallow copy; we'll deep-copy nested dicts on write
87	
88	    # Rename top-level faithfulness_score
89	    if "faithfulness_score" in out:
90	        legacy = out.pop("faithfulness_score")
91	        eval_block = dict(out.get("evaluator_output") or {})
92	        eval_block["legacy_faithfulness"] = legacy
93	        eval_block["legacy_note"] = LEGACY_NOTE
94	        eval_block.setdefault("external_evaluator_grade", None)
95	        out["evaluator_output"] = eval_block
96	        changes.append("moved top-level faithfulness_score -> evaluator_output.legacy_faithfulness")
97	
98	    # Rename quality_metrics.faithfulness_score
99	    qm = out.get("quality_metrics")
100	    if isinstance(qm, dict) and "faithfulness_score" in qm:
101	        qm = dict(qm)
102	        legacy = qm.pop("faithfulness_score")
103	        qm["_legacy_faithfulness_score"] = legacy
104	        qm["_legacy_note"] = LEGACY_NOTE
105	        out["quality_metrics"] = qm
106	        changes.append("renamed quality_metrics.faithfulness_score -> _legacy_faithfulness_score")
107	
108	    # Preserve hallucination_audit under legacy name if present
109	    if "hallucination_audit" in out:
110	        audit = out.pop("hallucination_audit")
111	        out["_legacy_hallucination_audit"] = audit
112	        changes.append("moved hallucination_audit -> _legacy_hallucination_audit (pre-rebuild NLI audit preserved for reference only)")
113	
114	    # Set mode label if missing
115	    if "mode_label" not in out:
116	        out["mode_label"] = LEGACY_MODE_LABEL
117	        changes.append("set mode_label = 'Legacy run, pre-honest-rebuild'")
118	
119	    # Migration marker
120	    out[MIGRATION_MARKER] = {
121	        "migrated_at": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
122	        "migration_script": "scripts/migrate_old_runs.py",
123	        "plan": "C:/Users/msn/.claude/plans/lovely-finding-firefly.md",
124	        "changes": changes,
125	    }
126	    return out, changes
127	
128	
129	def process_file(
130	    src_path: Path,
131	    archive_dir: Path,
132	    dry_run: bool,
133	) -> dict[str, Any]:
134	    """Process a single JSON file. Returns a status record."""
135	    status: dict[str, Any] = {
136	        "path": str(src_path),
137	        "action": "unknown",
138	        "changes": [],
139	        "error": None,
140	    }
141	    try:
142	        data = json.loads(src_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
143	    except Exception as exc:
144	        status["action"] = "error_reading"
145	        status["error"] = f"{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}"
146	        return status
147	
148	    if _is_already_migrated(data):
149	        status["action"] = "skip_already_migrated"
150	        return status
151	    if not _needs_migration(data):
152	        status["action"] = "skip_no_legacy_fields"
153	        return status
154	
155	    migrated, changes = migrate_record(data)
156	    status["changes"] = changes
157	
158	    if dry_run:
159	        status["action"] = "would_migrate"
160	        return status
161	
162	    # Archive original
163	    archive_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
164	    archived_path = archive_dir / src_path.name
165	    shutil.copy2(str(src_path), str(archived_path))
166	
167	    # Write migrated file in place
168	    src_path.write_text(
169	        json.dumps(migrated, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n",
170	        encoding="utf-8",
171	    )
172	    status["action"] = "migrated"
173	    status["archived_to"] = str(archived_path)
174	    return status
175	
176	
177	def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
178	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
179	    ap.add_argument(
180	        "--path",
181	        default="outputs/polaris_graph",
182	        help="directory containing run JSONs (default: outputs/polaris_graph)",
183	    )
184	    ap.add_argument(
185	        "--archive-dir",
186	        default="outputs/_archive_pre_rebuild",
187	        help="directory to archive pre-migration originals (default: outputs/_archive_pre_rebuild)",
188	    )
189	    ap.add_argument(
190	        "--dry-run",
191	        action="store_true",
192	        help="do not modify any files; print what would be done",
193	    )
194	    ap.add_argument(
195	        "--verbose", "-v",
196	        action="store_true",
197	        help="print per-file status lines",
198	    )
199	    args = ap.parse_args(argv)
200	
201	    src_dir = Path(args.path).resolve()
202	    archive_dir = Path(args.archive_dir).resolve()
203	
204	    if not src_dir.exists():
205	        print(f"[migrate_old_runs] source path does not exist: {src_dir}", file=sys.stderr)
206	        return 2
207	
208	    json_files = sorted(p for p in src_dir.glob("*.json") if p.is_file())
209	    if not json_files:
210	        print(f"[migrate_old_runs] no JSON files found in {src_dir}")
211	        return 0
212	
213	    print(
214	        f"[migrate_old_runs] scanning {len(json_files)} JSON files in {src_dir}"
215	        f" (dry_run={args.dry_run})"
216	    )
217	
218	    results = []
219	    for path in json_files:
220	        status = process_file(path, archive_dir, args.dry_run)
221	        results.append(status)
222	        if args.verbose or status["action"] in {"error_reading", "migrated", "would_migrate"}:
223	            line = f"  [{status['action']}] {path.name}"
224	            if status.get("error"):
225	                line += f"  error: {status['error']}"
226	            print(line)
227	
228	    # Counts
229	    by_action: dict[str, int] = {}
230	    for r in results:
231	        by_action[r["action"]] = by_action.get(r["action"], 0) + 1
232	    print(f"[migrate_old_runs] summary: {by_action}")
233	
234	    # Manifest (even in dry-run; it's just a record, doesn't modify source files)
235	    if not args.dry_run:
236	        archive_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
237	        manifest_path = archive_dir / "_migration_manifest.json"
238	        existing = []
239	        if manifest_path.exists():
240	            try:
241	                existing = json.loads(manifest_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
242	            except Exception:
243	                existing = []
244	        existing.append({
245	            "run_at": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
246	            "source_dir": str(src_dir),
247	            "results": results,
248	            "summary": by_action,
249	        })
250	        manifest_path.write_text(
251	            json.dumps(existing, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n",
252	            encoding="utf-8",
253	        )
254	        print(f"[migrate_old_runs] manifest: {manifest_path}")
255	
256	    return 0
257	
258	
259	if __name__ == "__main__":
260	    sys.exit(main())

1	"""Monitor a running POLARIS pipeline — polls /api/snapshot every 30s."""
2	import json
3	import time
4	import urllib.request
5	from datetime import datetime
6	
7	BASE = "http://localhost:8765"
8	INTERVAL = 30
9	
10	
11	def fetch_json(url):
12	    try:
13	        with urllib.request.urlopen(url, timeout=10) as r:
14	            return json.loads(r.read().decode("utf-8"))
15	    except Exception as e:
16	        return {"error": str(e)}
17	
18	
19	def main():
20	    print("=" * 70)
21	    print("  POLARIS Pipeline Monitor — polling every %ds" % INTERVAL)
22	    print("=" * 70)
23	
24	    prev_events = 0
25	    while True:
26	        now = datetime.now().strftime("%H:%M:%S")
27	        status = fetch_json(BASE + "/api/research/status")
28	        snap = fetch_json(BASE + "/api/snapshot")
29	
30	        if "error" in snap and "error" in status:
31	            print("[%s] ERROR: Server unreachable" % now)
32	            time.sleep(INTERVAL)
33	            continue
34	
35	        running = status.get("running", False)
36	        total_ev = snap.get("total_event_count", 0)
37	        new_ev = total_ev - prev_events
38	        prev_events = total_ev
39	
40	        s = snap.get("stats", {})
41	        ec = s.get("event_counts", {})
42	        nd = s.get("node_durations_ms", {})
43	
44	        # Determine current phase from last node_start
45	        ebt = snap.get("events_by_type", {})
46	        node_starts = ebt.get("node_start", [])
47	        current_node = node_starts[-1].get("node", "?") if node_starts else "?"
48	        node_ends = ebt.get("node_end", [])
49	        ended_nodes = set(e.get("node", "") for e in node_ends)
50	
51	        # Evidence count
52	        evidence_count = s.get("total_evidence", 0)
53	
54	        # Cost
55	        cost = s.get("total_cost_usd", 0)
56	
57	        # Searches
58	        searches = ec.get("search_result", 0)
59	
60	        # LLM calls
61	        llm_calls = ec.get("llm_call", 0)
62	
63	        # Fetches
64	        fetches = ec.get("fetch", 0)
65	
66	        # Verification verdicts
67	        verdicts = ec.get("verification_batch", 0)
68	
69	        # STORM
70	        storm = ec.get("storm_transcript", 0)
71	
72	        # Iteration
73	        iters = ec.get("iteration_decision", 0)
74	
75	        # Node durations summary
76	        phase_times = []
77	        for node, ms in nd.items():
78	            phase_times.append("%s=%.0fs" % (node, ms / 1000))
79	
80	        status_icon = "RUN" if running else "DONE"
81	        if not running and status.get("error"):
82	            status_icon = "ERR"

90	                storm, verdicts, iters, cost,
91	            )
92	        )
93	        if phase_times:
94	            print("         phases: %s" % " | ".join(phase_times))
95	
96	        if not running:
97	            result_path = status.get("result_path")
98	            error = status.get("error")
99	            if error:
100	                print("\n  PIPELINE ERROR: %s" % error)
101	            elif result_path:
102	                print("\n  PIPELINE COMPLETE — result: %s" % result_path)
103	            else:
104	                print("\n  PIPELINE FINISHED (no result path)")
105	            break
106	
107	        time.sleep(INTERVAL)
108	
109	    print("=" * 70)
110	    print("  Monitor stopped.")
111	    print("=" * 70)

4	WHY THIS EXISTS (the I-arch-005 lesson)
5	---------------------------------------
6	``scripts/pipeline_diced_preflight.py`` checks the pipeline-STAGE §-1.3 invariants (weight-not-
7	filter / consolidate-not-drop / basket-faithfulness) against a banked corpus_snapshot. This harness
8	checks the OTHER failure class — the boring run-CONFIG settings that SILENTLY KILL a paid run before a
9	single useful token is produced:
10	
11	  * committed + Codex-approved != WIRED on the run path (I-arch-005): a fix that is real in the tree
12	    but DEAD because the flag that activates it is default-off and only the Gate-B slate turns it on.
13	    A direct ``run_honest_sweep_r3.py`` WITHOUT ``--pathB-gate`` never applies the slate -> the breadth
14	    enrichment is OFF -> a narrow report.

16	  * a timeout ordered wrong -> a healthy section is guillotined mid-write, or the 4-role seam tears.
17	  * a §-1.3-BANNED cap pinned ON -> the funnel silently re-asserts and breadth collapses.
18	  * a silent degrade (credibility tiering / relevance-gate demote) NOT disclosed to the manifest.

20	THE RUN CONFIG SOURCE OF TRUTH (read, never re-declared) + THE CANONICAL LAUNCH PATH
21	------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
22	The paid run's ONLY slate-applying entrypoint is ``scripts/dr_benchmark/run_gate_b.py`` (launched
23	DIRECTLY: ``python scripts/dr_benchmark/run_gate_b.py --only <slug> [--resume]``). Its ``main()`` and
24	``run_gate_b_query`` call ``apply_full_capability_benchmark_slate`` (force/floor) + ``enable_four_role_mode``.
25	DO NOT launch the paid run via ``scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py --pathB-gate``: that wrapper
26	(``pathB_runner.gate_around_question``) is a capture/preflight gate ONLY — it does NOT apply the slate,
27	so ``PG_BREADTH_ENRICHMENT_ENABLED`` (and the rest of the slate) stays default-OFF -> a NARROW report.
28	The D-1 launch-path check verifies the slate IS applied by run_gate_b.py and discloses this loudly.

31	force-on / force-exact / required flag sets, ``benchmark_verifier_lineup``, ``load_lock``,
32	``assert_four_role_families_distinct``) and re-derives the EFFECTIVE config the SAME way the run will —
33	a faithful READ-ONLY replica of ``apply_full_capability_benchmark_slate``'s FORCE / FLOOR / setdefault
34	semantics. It NEVER re-hardcodes a value (that would be exactly the drift the preflight exists to catch).
35	It reads the .env-merged env WHILE resolving (fidelity) but SNAPSHOTS + RESTORES ``os.environ`` around
36	the run_gate_b import so the process env is left BYTE-IDENTICAL — that import transitively runs
37	``load_dotenv`` AND ``src/_polaris_native_thread_safety.apply_native_thread_safety_clamp`` (os.environ
38	.setdefault of the native thread-pool knobs), both of which would otherwise mutate the env. It loads NO
39	model and makes NO paid LLM call.

41	BUILD-ONLY / NO SPEND
42	---------------------
43	The ONLY network this harness performs is a FREE, UNAUTHENTICATED ``GET`` to the OpenRouter catalog
44	(``/api/v1/models`` + ``/api/v1/models/{slug}/endpoints``) to confirm each role's slug is served and the
45	Judge has enough providers (anti-429). That GET bills nothing. It is OPT-OUT (``--no-ping``) and degrades
46	to ``LIVE-PENDING`` (never RED, never a harness error) on any network failure. NOTHING here launches a
47	heavy run, loads a model, or makes a completion call.

49	STATIC vs LIVE-PING (honest labelling)

51	Each check is labelled ``STATIC`` (provable from config alone, deterministic) or ``LIVE-PING`` (only a

60	    python scripts/operational_readiness_preflight.py                 # static + free /models ping + diced shell
61	    python scripts/operational_readiness_preflight.py --no-ping       # static only (no network)

63	    python scripts/operational_readiness_preflight.py --json out.json  # machine-readable sidecar
64	    python scripts/operational_readiness_preflight.py --require-live-ping  # an unreachable /models ping => NO-GO

633	def ping_openrouter_catalog(slugs: Mapping[str, str], judge_slug: str, th: Thresholds) -> dict:
634	    """FREE unauthenticated GET against the OpenRouter catalog — NO spend, NO completion. Returns a
635	    structured result; ANY network/parse failure -> {'reachable': False, ...} (degrades, never raises)."""
636	    result: dict = {"reachable": False, "served": {}, "judge_providers": None, "error": None}
637	    try:
638	        import httpx  # noqa: PLC0415
639	
640	        base = th.ping_base_url.rstrip("/")
641	        with httpx.Client(timeout=th.ping_timeout_seconds) as client:
642	            resp = client.get(f"{base}/models")
643	            resp.raise_for_status()
644	            data = resp.json().get("data")
645	            if not isinstance(data, list):
646	                raise ValueError("/models body has no `data` list")
647	            catalog: set[str] = set()
648	            for entry in data:
649	                if not isinstance(entry, dict):
650	                    continue
651	                for key in ("id", "canonical_slug"):
652	                    val = entry.get(key)
653	                    if val:
654	                        catalog.add(val)
655	            result["served"] = {role: (slug in catalog) for role, slug in slugs.items()}
656	            # provider count for the Judge (anti-429) = len of /models/{slug}/endpoints.
657	            ep = client.get(f"{base}/models/{judge_slug}/endpoints")
658	            ep.raise_for_status()
659	            ep_data = ep.json().get("data")
660	            endpoints = ep_data.get("endpoints") if isinstance(ep_data, dict) else None
661	            if isinstance(endpoints, list):
662	                result["judge_providers"] = len(endpoints)
663	            result["reachable"] = True
664	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — any failure degrades to LIVE-PENDING, never RED/harness-error
665	        result["error"] = f"{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}"
666	    return result

749	def check_d4_timeouts(cfg: Mapping[str, str], th: Thresholds) -> list[CheckResult]:
750	    gen = _to_int(cfg.get("PG_GENERATOR_LLM_TIMEOUT_SECONDS"))
751	    section = _to_int(cfg.get("PG_SECTION_WALLCLOCK_SECONDS"))
752	    run_wall = _to_int(cfg.get("PG_RUN_WALL_CLOCK_SEC"))
753	    seam = _to_int(cfg.get("PG_FOUR_ROLE_SEAM_TIMEOUT_SECONDS"))
754	    out: list[CheckResult] = []
755	
756	    if None in (gen, section, run_wall):
757	        out.append(CheckResult(
758	            "D-4.ordering", "gen-call-wall < per-section budget < run wall", RED, STATIC,
759	            f"un-resolvable timeout(s): gen={gen} section={section} run_wall={run_wall}",
760	        ))
761	    else:
762	        ordering_ok = gen < section < run_wall
763	        out.append(_ok(
764	            "D-4.ordering", "gen-call-wall < per-section budget < run wall", ordering_ok, STATIC,
765	            f"gen={gen} < section={section} < run_wall={run_wall} -> "
766	            f"{'ordered (no healthy section guillotined mid-write)' if ordering_ok else 'INVERTED -> mid-write truncation'}",
767	        ))
768	
769	    if seam is None or run_wall is None:
770	        out.append(CheckResult(
771	            "D-4.seam", "4-role seam wall raised for the big/slow kimi judge", RED, STATIC,
772	            f"un-resolvable: seam={seam} run_wall={run_wall}",
773	        ))
774	    else:
775	        # Binding lower bound = max(the §9.1.8 sane floor for the slow kimi judge, a single gen call) so
776	        # the seam is never tinier than a generation call AND is generous for the per-claim 4-role burst;
777	        # binding upper bound = the run wall (the seam must terminate inside it). seam-vs-section is
778	        # advisory: the locked full slate legitimately runs seam(7200) < section(9000) because the
779	        # section wall sizes ONE 64000-token section's generation while the seam covers whole-report D8.
780	        lower = max(th.seam_floor_seconds, gen or 0)
781	        seam_ok = (seam >= lower) and (seam <= run_wall)
782	        out.append(_ok(
783	            "D-4.seam", "4-role seam wall sane (>= floor & gen call, <= run wall)", seam_ok, STATIC,
784	            f"seam={seam} (floor {th.seam_floor_seconds}, gen-call {gen}, lower-bound {lower}) "

1	"""Production-faithful cp3 -> S4 outline lab bank adapter (I-arch section-modular).
2	
3	Builds the offline S4 hamster bank (``bank_plan.json``) from REAL cp2 + cp3 checkpoints — NO
4	synthetic data (LAW II). Pool = cp3 basket members enriched with the REAL cp2 corpus row
5	(title / statement / tier). Clusters = cp3 multi-member baskets. ``same_work_groups`` carried
6	straight from cp3.
7	
8	Item 6a (I-arch s4-outline fix wave): bank rows now CARRY the cp2 content-integrity + topic + weight
9	STAMPS. The prior per-run adapter emitted only ``{evidence_id, title, statement, tier}``, DROPPING
10	the stamps, so the fail-open junk / off-topic / chrome predicates
11	(``junk_deletion_gate.is_row_deletable_offtopic`` and the content-integrity chrome predicate) could
12	never FIRE in the lab — a "Just a moment..." Cloudflare row or a confirmed off-topic source read as
13	normal evidence. Carrying the stamps lets the offline lab reproduce the production gate behaviour.
14	§-1.3.1: the gate KEEPS on any uncertainty (fail-open); a missing stamp is simply absent, which the
15	predicate reads as unjudged => KEEP.
16	
17	LAW VI: all paths are CLI args (no hardcoding). There are no fixed corpus paths in this module.
18	"""
19	from __future__ import annotations
20	
21	import argparse
22	import hashlib
23	import json
24	from pathlib import Path

26	# item 6a: the cp2 stamp fields the fail-open junk / off-topic / chrome predicates + the weight
27	# channel consume (``junk_deletion_gate`` reads these exact names: content_integrity_junk /
28	# content_integrity_class / topic_off_subject / topic_relevance_verdict / topic_offtopic_demoted /
29	# content_relevance_label / deletion_reason). ``line_screen`` / ``provenance_class`` are the S2
30	# line-screen stamps Fable named. ``url`` / ``source_url`` help the chrome predicate. Any field that
31	# is present on the cp2 row is carried verbatim onto the bank row; an absent field is simply not
32	# added (the predicates treat a missing stamp as unjudged => KEEP, §-1.3.1 fail-open).
33	_STAMP_FIELDS = (
34	    "content_integrity_junk",
35	    "content_integrity_class",
36	    "topic_off_subject",
37	    "topic_relevance_verdict",
38	    "topic_offtopic_demoted",

48	def _carry_stamps(dst: dict, src: dict) -> None:
49	    """Copy any present stamp field from the cp2 row ``src`` onto the bank row ``dst`` (item 6a)."""
50	    for field in _STAMP_FIELDS:
51	        if field in src and src.get(field) is not None:
52	            dst[field] = src[field]

54	
55	def build_bank(
56	    cp2_path: Path,
57	    cp3_path: Path,
58	    out_path: Path,
59	    *,
60	    deliverable: dict | None = None,
61	    scope: dict | None = None,
62	) -> dict:
63	    """Assemble the S4 bank from cp2 + cp3 and write ``out_path``. Returns the bank dict."""
64	    cp2 = json.loads(cp2_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
65	    _cp2_rows = cp2.get("evidence_for_gen", []) or []
66	    cp2_rows_total = len(_cp2_rows)  # item 6: the FULL cp2 candidate pool (pre-basket)
67	    by_id = {str(r.get("evidence_id", "")): r for r in _cp2_rows}
68

82	            ev_id = str(ev_id)

125	    # item 6: pool-delta disclosure. The bank pool = cp3 basket MEMBERS only; any cp2 candidate never
126	    # basketed by S3 silently vanishes from the S4 pool. Record the counts + the delta so the drop is
127	    # VISIBLE (never silent). VERIFY on the S3 wheel: prove cp3 basket membership covers 100% of its
128	    # cp2 input, OR that this delta equals the disclosed S2/S3 (off-topic/junk/chrome) deletions.
129	    # item 9: ``cp3_member_dedup_skipped`` = duplicate member references collapsed to one bank row.
130	    bank_rows = len(evidence)
131	    cp2_to_bank_delta = cp2_rows_total - bank_rows
132	    bank = {
133	        "question": question,
134	        "domain": domain,
135	        "cp3_sha": cp3_sha,
136	        "evidence": evidence,
137	        "clusters": clusters,
138	        "same_work_groups": same_work_groups,
139	        "pool_ev_ids": [e["evidence_id"] for e in evidence],
140	        # item 6 (pool-delta disclosure):
141	        "cp2_rows_total": cp2_rows_total,
142	        "bank_rows": bank_rows,
143	        "cp2_to_bank_delta": cp2_to_bank_delta,
144	        # item 9 (duplicate-ev_id disclosure): total dup member refs + distinct ev_ids affected.
145	        "cp3_member_dedup_skipped": len(dedup_skipped),
146	        "cp3_member_dedup_distinct": len(set(dedup_skipped)),
147	    }
148	    if deliverable is not None:
149	        bank["deliverable"] = deliverable
150	    if scope is not None:
151	        bank["scope"] = scope
152	
153	    out_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
154	    out_path.write_text(json.dumps(bank, ensure_ascii=False), encoding="utf-8")
155	    stamped_rows = sum(1 for e in evidence if any(f in e for f in _STAMP_FIELDS))
156	    print(f"wrote {out_path} bytes {out_path.stat().st_size}")
157	    print(f"evidence_rows {len(evidence)} clusters(multi>=2) {len(clusters)} "
158	          f"same_work_groups {len(same_work_groups)} missing_in_cp2 {len(missing)} "
159	          f"stamped_rows {stamped_rows}")
160	    # item 6/9: pool-delta + duplicate-ev_id disclosure (a cp2 candidate never basketed by S3 drops
161	    # out of the S4 pool; the delta must equal disclosed S2/S3 deletions — VERIFY on the S3 wheel).
162	    print(f"cp2_rows_total {cp2_rows_total} bank_rows {bank_rows} cp2_to_bank_delta {cp2_to_bank_delta} "
163	          f"cp3_member_dedup_skipped {len(dedup_skipped)} (distinct {len(set(dedup_skipped))})")
164	    print(f"cp3_sha {cp3_sha[:16]}")
165	    return bank
166	
167	
168	def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
169	    p = argparse.ArgumentParser(
170	        description="cp3 -> S4 outline lab bank adapter (carries cp2 content-integrity/topic stamps)"
171	    )
172	    p.add_argument("--cp2", required=True, type=Path, help="cp2 corpus snapshot JSON")
173	    p.add_argument("--cp3", required=True, type=Path, help="cp3 basket snapshot JSON")
174	    p.add_argument("--out", required=True, type=Path, help="bank_plan.json output path")
175	    p.add_argument("--deliverable", type=Path, default=None,
176	                   help="optional deliverable spec JSON (required_sections / tone / ...)")
177	    p.add_argument("--scope", type=Path, default=None, help="optional scope spec JSON")
178	    args = p.parse_args(argv)
179	    deliverable = (
180	        json.loads(args.deliverable.read_text(encoding="utf-8")) if args.deliverable else None
181	    )
182	    scope = json.loads(args.scope.read_text(encoding="utf-8")) if args.scope else None
183	    build_bank(args.cp2, args.cp3, args.out, deliverable=deliverable, scope=scope)
184	    return 0
185	
186	
187	if __name__ == "__main__":
188	    raise SystemExit(main())
